queue_recommendation: Review the post-rewrite Claim Discovery user spec from the beginning. Keep focus on product value, DDD-style shared vocabulary, binary/skill boundary, false-positive and false-negative handling, and whether prepared-but-not-executed eval evidence is honest.

items.decision: Needs opening rewrite: strengthen first-sentence user value, use bullets for the two-pass workflow, explain recall as accepting false positives to avoid missing in-scope declarations, avoid premature packet language, and restore proof-route bullets with examples.
Does this chunk make the scan boundary, in-scope FN bug boundary, out-of-scope narrative gap, and non-verdict status clear?
Does this chunk show concrete binary heuristics and sentence-level duplicate handling with enough executable evidence?
Does this chunk make the skill's FP/FN curation behavior visible while honestly saying the eval has not run?
Do the next-action groups and prepared skill-eval section explain what happens next without confusing user workflow with maintainer proof?
